intro:
There is not enough information available about Ochnella boiviniana. However, I can provide information on a similar plant called Ochna moensis.

Ochna moensis is a plant species in the family Ochnaceae. It is traditionally used in medicine in various parts of the world, particularly in the Pacific region, for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ochna moensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Ochna moensis is commonly prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues.
